assertions.evidence: All 14 modules identified in lecture-content.html and extracted to cheatsheet.md. Table of Contents shows all modules present.
Each module has key concepts, syntax patterns, and important distinctions extracted. Examples: HTTP protocol, DNS, semantic elements, flexbox, Object.keys(), etc.
Internet section contains: HTTP Protocol, Client-Server Model, DNS, File Paths (Absolute/Relative/Root-Relative). URLs shown as examples in file paths table.
HTML section contains: Document Structure (DOCTYPE, html, head, body), Meta Tags, Semantic Elements (header, nav, main, article, section, footer), Links, Images, Lists.
CSS section contains: Selectors (element, class, ID, descendant, pseudo-class), Box Model, Box Sizing, Display Property, Specificity, Colors. CSS Layout section has Flexbox and Positioning.
JS Basics section contains: Variables (const, let, var), Data Types (string, number, boolean, null, undefined, object), Operators, Type Coercion gotchas.
JS Control Flow section contains: Comparisons (=== vs ==), Logical Operators (&&, ||, !), Ternary Operator.
JS Functions section contains: Function Declaration, Function Expression, Arrow Functions, First-Class Functions.
JS Objects section contains: Object Literals, Property Access (dot and bracket notation), Object Methods (Object.assign(), Object.keys()).
JS DOM section contains: Query Methods (getElementById, querySelector, querySelectorAll), Modifying Elements, Traversing, Styles & Classes.
JS Events section contains: addEventListener, Event Object, Event Propagation (stopPropagation, preventDefault), Common Events table.
JS Classes section contains: Class Declaration, Constructor, Inheritance (extends, super), Static Methods.
HTML Forms section contains: Basic Form, Input Types table, Validation Attributes, Form Handling with preventDefault.
CSS Responsive section contains: Viewport Meta Tag, Media Queries, Common Breakpoints, Responsive Images (max-width: 100%).
Web APIs section contains: localStorage (with JSON.stringify/parse), sessionStorage, XMLHttpRequest, Cookies, Storage Comparison table.

All 14 modules from Table of Contents present in cheatsheet: Internet Basics, HTML, CSS Fundamentals, CSS Layout, CSS Responsive, JavaScript Basics, JavaScript Control Flow, JavaScript Functions, JavaScript Objects, JavaScript DOM, JavaScript Events, JavaScript Classes, HTML Forms, Web APIs.

assertions.reason: Cheatsheet not pushed to GitHub. git status shows 'Your branch is ahead of origin/main by 13 commits'. push-cheatsheet feature is pending and must be completed to fulfill this assertion.
